title: Analysis plan
text: Estimate associations between child diet exposures and child metabolite outcomes at visit 6 and visit 7, including a prospective conditional follow-up analysis from visit 6 to visit 7 and a repeated-measures mixed model across visits 6 and 7.

title: Analysis plan
text: Estimate associations between child diet exposures and child metabolite outcomes at visit 6 and visit 7, including a prospective conditional follow-up analysis from visit 6 to visit 7 and a repeated-measures mixed model across visits 6 and 7.

title: Analysis plan
text:
2 Scope

Primary exposures: child diet metrics (nutrient E%, diet quality variables).
Primary outcomes: metabolite-level outcomes.
Secondary outcomes: pathway or cluster scores.

title: Analysis plan
text:
3 Data processing steps

Build visit-specific analysis sets (Filter6==1, Filter7==1).
Validate diet variables and units at each visit.
Log-transform skewed metabolite outcomes when required.
Standardize outcomes within visit.
Generate visit-specific missingness and sparsity tables.
Run covariate balance tests across intervention groups (Kruskal-Wallis for continuous, Fisher exact for discrete covariates including ordinal and nominal categorical) and apply BH correction.

title: Analysis plan
text:
4 Covariate strategy
Visit 6 core block:

Intervention, MPrimipara, MUniEdu, MGDMOGTT1OR2Fi, CGender, CWeightBirth, Bfdurationm

Visit 7 core block:

Intervention, MPrimipara, MUniEdu, MGDMOGTT1OR2Fi, CGender, CWeightBirth, Bfdurationm

Prospective baseline block:

Intervention, MPrimipara, MUniEdu, MGDMOGTT1OR2Fi, CGender, CWeightBirth, Bfdurationm

Repeated-measures core block:

Intervention, MPrimipara, MUniEdu, MGDMOGTT1OR2Fi, CGender, CWeightBirth, Bfdurationm

Sensitivity block:

MPRSmoke, Premature, Bfdurationm, CSGA, CMacrosomy, CHealthy6, CHealthy7

Covariates are pre-specified. Test results are reported and are not used as the sole inclusion rule.

title: Analysis plan
text:
5 Model plan

Visit-specific models:

lm(Y_v6 ~ E_v6 + core_v6)
lm(Y_v7 ~ E_v7 + core_v7_lean)

Longitudinal conditional model:

lm(Y_v7 ~ E_v6 + Y_v6 + baseline_covariates)
fit separately for each metabolite so each visit-7 metabolite is conditioned on its own visit-6 baseline value

Repeated-measures mixed model:

lmer(Y_it ~ E_it * visit_t + repeated_core_covariates + (1|child))
E_it is harmonized within-child exposure at visit 6 or visit 7
sensitivity healthy-diet variables are harmonized as time-varying covariates for expanded sensitivity models

title: Analysis plan
text:
6 Multiplicity

Apply Benjamini-Hochberg correction across metabolites, with the adjustment scope defined by model family.
Concurrent outputs apply BH within each exposure-specific result set.
Prospective outputs apply BH within each exposure-specific result set.
Repeated-measures outputs apply BH separately within each exposure and coefficient family.
Report raw p, adjusted q, effect size, and confidence interval.

title: Analysis plan
text:
7 Deliverables

Visit-specific effect tables.
Prospective conditional model table.
Repeated-measures mixed-model table with main-effect and diet-by-visit interaction terms.
Sensitivity comparison table.
Model-status summary with complete-case ranges and skipped/failed reasons.
Figures: effect-size plots, repeated-measures status plots, and prospective overlap summaries.

title: Analysis plan
text:
8 Tools

Data: haven, readxl, dplyr, tidyr
Models: stats, limma, lmerTest, broom.mixed
Figures: ggplot2, patchwork
DAG and adjustment checks: dagitty

Show code
source("analysis_data_utils.R")
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)

has_venn <- requireNamespace("ggVennDiagram", quietly = TRUE)

analysis_obj <- prepare_analysis_data(
  mae_path = "../data/MAE_original.rds",
  visits = c("visit_6", "visit_7")
)

df <- analysis_obj$data
metabolite_cols <- analysis_obj$metabolite_cols
cluster_map <- analysis_obj$cluster_map
raw_df <- extract_long_from_mae(
  analysis_obj$mae,
  visits = c("visit_6", "visit_7")
)

visit_label <- function(v) dplyr::case_when(
  v == 6L ~ "2 Years",
  v == 7L ~ "5-6 Years",
  TRUE ~ paste("Visit", v)
)

pretty_var <- function(x) {
  x <- gsub("CEPercent", "Percent ", x)
  x <- gsub("CFibtot", "Fibre ", x)
  x <- gsub("C_", "", x)
  x <- gsub("DietQuality", "Diet Quality ", x)
  x <- gsub("CHO", "Carbohydrate", x)
  x <- gsub("Pro", "Protein", x)
  x <- gsub("FAn3", "n-3 fatty acids", x)
  x <- gsub("FAn6", "n-6 fatty acids", x)
  x <- gsub("SFA", "SFA", x)
  x <- gsub("MUFA", "MUFA", x)
  x <- gsub("PUFA", "PUFA", x)
  x <- gsub("Diet6", "Diet", x)
  x <- gsub("Diet7", "Diet", x)
  x <- gsub("_6", "", x, fixed = TRUE)
  x <- gsub("_7", "", x, fixed = TRUE)
  x <- gsub("_", " ", x, fixed = TRUE)
  x <- gsub("\\s+", " ", trimws(x))
  tools::toTitleCase(tolower(x))
}

source("analysis_data_utils.R")
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)

has_venn <- requireNamespace("ggVennDiagram", quietly = TRUE)

analysis_obj <- prepare_analysis_data(
  mae_path = "../data/MAE_original.rds",
  visits = c("visit_6", "visit_7")
)

df <- analysis_obj$data
metabolite_cols <- analysis_obj$metabolite_cols
cluster_map <- analysis_obj$cluster_map
raw_df <- extract_long_from_mae(
  analysis_obj$mae,
  visits = c("visit_6", "visit_7")
)

visit_label <- function(v) dplyr::case_when(
  v == 6L ~ "2 Years",
  v == 7L ~ "5-6 Years",
  TRUE ~ paste("Visit", v)
)

pretty_var <- function(x) {
  x <- gsub("CEPercent", "Percent ", x)
  x <- gsub("CFibtot", "Fibre ", x)
  x <- gsub("C_", "", x)
  x <- gsub("DietQuality", "Diet Quality ", x)
  x <- gsub("CHO", "Carbohydrate", x)
  x <- gsub("Pro", "Protein", x)
  x <- gsub("FAn3", "n-3 fatty acids", x)
  x <- gsub("FAn6", "n-6 fatty acids", x)
  x <- gsub("SFA", "SFA", x)
  x <- gsub("MUFA", "MUFA", x)
  x <- gsub("PUFA", "PUFA", x)
  x <- gsub("Diet6", "Diet", x)
  x <- gsub("Diet7", "Diet", x)

3 Visit overlap


Show code
id_sets <- split(df$StudyID, df$visit_num)
id_sets <- lapply(id_sets, function(x) sort(unique(as.character(x))))
id_sets <- id_sets[order(as.integer(names(id_sets)))]
names(id_sets) <- vapply(as.integer(names(id_sets)), visit_label, character(1))

if (!has_venn || length(id_sets) < 2) {
  ggplot() +
    annotate("text", x = 0, y = 0, label = "Visit-overlap plot unavailable") +
    theme_void()
} else {
  ggVennDiagram::ggVennDiagram(id_sets, label = "both") +
    ggplot2::scale_fill_distiller(palette = "RdBu") +
    ggplot2::scale_x_continuous(expand = ggplot2::expansion(mult = 0.18)) +
    ggplot2::theme_void(base_size = 12) +
    ggplot2::theme(
      plot.margin = ggplot2::margin(5.5, 5.5, 5.5, 28)
    ) +
    ggplot2::labs(
      title = "Child overlap across ages",
      fill = "Count"
    )
}

  dv <- df %>% filter(visit_num == v)

}))

title: Data summary
text:
6 Global structure (PCA) with top loadings


Show code
pca_details <- compute_pca_details(
  df = df,
  feature_cols = metabolite_cols,
  group_cols = c("visit"),
  min_feature_obs = 0.7,
  top_n_loadings = 5L
)

if (is.null(pca_details)) {
  ggplot() +
    annotate("text", x = 0, y = 0, label = "PCA unavailable: insufficient complete feature matrix") +
    theme_void()
} else {
  scores <- pca_details$scores
  scores$visit <- factor(gsub("_", " ", scores$visit), levels = sort(unique(gsub("_", " ", scores$visit))))
  visit_counts <- scores %>%
    count(visit, name = "n") %>%
    mutate(label = paste0(visit, " (n=", n, ")"))
  label_map <- setNames(visit_counts$label, as.character(visit_counts$visit))
  legend_labels <- unname(label_map[levels(scores$visit)])
  top_loadings_plot <- pca_details$top_loadings
  score_scale <- max(abs(scores$PC1), abs(scores$PC2), na.rm = TRUE)

  has_ellipse <- scores %>%
    count(visit, name = "n") %>%
    summarise(ok = all(n >= 3) & n() >= 2) %>%
    pull(ok)

  p <- ggplot(scores, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, color = visit)) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.7, size = 1.7) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
    coord_equal() +
    scale_color_brewer(palette = "Set1", labels = legend_labels, drop = FALSE) +
    labs(
      title = "PCA across child ages",
      x = paste0("PC1 (", round(100 * pca_details$var_explained[1], 1), "%)"),
      y = paste0("PC2 (", round(100 * pca_details$var_explained[2], 1), "%)"),
      color = "Visit"
    ) +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5),
      legend.position = "top",
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
    )

  if (isTRUE(has_ellipse)) {
    p <- p + stat_ellipse(linewidth = 0.8, alpha = 0.7)
  }

  p +
    geom_segment(
      data = top_loadings_plot,
      aes(x = 0, y = 0, xend = PC1_scaled, yend = PC2_scaled),
      inherit.aes = FALSE,
      color = "black",
      linewidth = 0.45,
      arrow = arrow(length = grid::unit(0.18, "cm"))
    ) +
    geom_text(
      data = top_loadings_plot,
      aes(x = PC1_scaled, y = PC2_scaled, label = feature),
      inherit.aes = FALSE,
      size = 2.8,
      color = "black",
      check_overlap = TRUE,
      nudge_y = 0.02 * score_scale
    )
}

if (!is.null(pca_details)) {
  loading_plot_tbl <- pca_details$loadings %>%
    select(feature, PC1, PC2) %>%
    pivot_longer(cols = c(PC1, PC2), names_to = "component", values_to = "loading") %>%
    group_by(component) %>%
    slice_max(order_by = abs(loading), n = 10, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
    ungroup() %>%
    mutate(
      effect = ifelse(loading >= 0, "positive", "negative"),
      feature = reorder(feature, abs(loading))
    )

  ggplot(loading_plot_tbl, aes(x = abs(loading), y = feature, fill = effect)) +
    geom_col(width = 0.85) +
    facet_wrap(~component, scales = "free_y") +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c(negative = "#4B00FF", positive = "#FF1A1A")) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 10) +
    labs(
      title = "Top PCA loadings by component",
      x = "Value",
      y = "Feature",
      fill = "Effect"
    )
}

title: Covariate and model specification
Covariate and model specification
Covariate and model specification
text:
2 Analysis question
Association between child diet exposures and child metabolite outcomes at visit 6 and visit 7.

title: Covariate and model specification
text:
3 Covariate specification

3.1 Covariate selection framework
Covariate selection is pre-specified and DAG-informed:

The primary adjustment set is fixed in advance for each visit.
Balance tests and missingness summaries are reported.
Collinearity diagnostics are reported to flag potentially unstable expanded adjustment sets.
Secondary models add sensitivity covariates.



Show code
graph LR
    A[Covariate Candidate] --> B{In Core DAG?}
    B -- Yes --> C[Status: keep_core]
    B -- No --> D{In Sensitivity Block?}
    D -- Yes --> E{Screening Signal?<br>p < 0.20 OR Beta Change > 10%}
    E -- Yes --> F[Status: keep_sensitivity]
    E -- No --> G[Status: consider_drop]
    D -- No --> G





graph LR
    A[Covariate Candidate] --> B{In Core DAG?}
    B -- Yes --> C[Status: keep_core]
    B -- No --> D{In Sensitivity Block?}
    D -- Yes --> E{Screening Signal?<br>p < 0.20 OR Beta Change > 10%}
    E -- Yes --> F[Status: keep_sensitivity]
    E -- No --> G[Status: consider_drop]
    D -- No --> G








3.2 Group balance tests across intervention arms






Why this matters: Balance Diagnostics



These tests check whether covariates are balanced across the intervention arms. Because this study evaluates a randomized trial context, minor imbalances can arise by chance. Note that standard guidelines discourage using p-values alone to drop covariates—pre-specified confounders are retained regardless of balance test significance.


Covariate balance diagnostics across intervention groups:

Kruskal-Wallis for continuous covariates.
Fisher exact for discrete covariates (ordinal and nominal categorical).
BH adjusted p-values across all visits in the combined table.

These are covariate-intervention association tests used as balance diagnostics. They are not outcome-association tests, and their p-values are not used alone to choose adjustment covariates. The intervention variable itself is the grouping variable and is therefore not included as a balance-test covariate.


Show code
run_covariate_test <- function(df, group_var, covariate, test_method) {
  out <- data.frame(
    var = covariate,
    test = test_method,
    p_value = NA_real_,
    p_adj_bh = NA_real_,
    n_groups = NA_integer_,
    note = NA_character_,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )

  if (!covariate %in% names(df)) {
    out$note <- "covariate_missing_in_data"
    return(out)
  }
  if (!group_var %in% names(df)) {
    out$note <- "group_var_missing_in_data"
    return(out)
  }
  if (!test_method %in% c("anova", "kruskal_wallis", "fisher_or_chisq")) {
    out$note <- "invalid_test_method"
    return(out)
  }

  x <- df[[covariate]]
  g <- df[[group_var]]
  keep <- !is.na(x) & !is.na(g)
  if (!any(keep)) {
    out$note <- "no_complete_cases"
    return(out)
  }

  x <- x[keep]
  g <- as.factor(g[keep])
  out$n_groups <- nlevels(g)

  if (out$n_groups < 2L) {
    out$note <- "fewer_than_two_groups"
    return(out)
  }
  if (length(unique(x)) < 2L) {
    out$note <- "constant_covariate"
    return(out)
  }

  if (identical(test_method, "anova")) {
    out$p_value <- tryCatch({
      fit <- aov(as.numeric(x) ~ g)
      summary(fit)[[1]][["Pr(>F)"]][1]
    }, error = function(e) NA_real_)
  } else if (identical(test_method, "kruskal_wallis")) {
    out$test <- "kruskal_wallis"
    y <- if (is.character(x) || is.factor(x)) as.numeric(factor(x)) else as.numeric(x)
    out$p_value <- tryCatch(kruskal.test(y ~ g)$p.value, error = function(e) NA_real_)
  } else {
    x_cat <- as.factor(x)
    if (nlevels(x_cat) < 2L) {
      out$note <- "fewer_than_two_levels"
      return(out)
    }

    tbl <- table(g, x_cat)
    if (any(dim(tbl) < 2L)) {
      out$note <- "invalid_contingency_table"
      return(out)
    }

    out$p_value <- tryCatch({
      out$test <- "fisher_exact"
      fisher.test(tbl)$p.value
    }, error = function(e) NA_real_)
  }

  out$note <- ifelse(is.na(out$p_value), "test_failed", "ok")
  out
}

run_group_balance_panel <- function(df, group_var, covariates, panel_name) {
  res <- bind_rows(lapply(seq_len(nrow(covariates)), function(i) {
    run_covariate_test(
      df = df,
      group_var = group_var,
      covariate = covariates$var[i],
      test_method = covariates$test[i]
    )
  }))
  res$panel <- panel_name
  res %>% arrange(p_value, .by_group = FALSE)
}

pretty_test <- function(x) dplyr::case_when(
  x == "anova" ~ "ANOVA",
  x == "kruskal_wallis" ~ "Kruskal-Wallis",
  x == "fisher_exact" ~ "Fisher exact",
  TRUE ~ x
)
format_p <- function(x) {
  ifelse(
    is.na(x),
    "",
    ifelse(x < 0.001, "<0.001", formatC(x, format = "f", digits = 3))
  )
}
format_p2 <- function(x) ifelse(is.na(x), "", formatC(x, format = "f", digits = 2))

plot_balance_var <- function(df, group_var, var_name, test_name, panel_label) {
  x <- df[[var_name]]
  g <- as.factor(df[[group_var]])
  keep <- !is.na(x) & !is.na(g)

  if (!any(keep)) return(NULL)

  dat <- data.frame(
    group = as.factor(g[keep]),
    value = x[keep],
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )

  if (test_name %in% c("anova", "kruskal_wallis")) {
    dat$value <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(dat$value))
    ggplot(dat, aes(x = group, y = value)) +
      geom_boxplot(outlier.alpha = 0.4, width = 0.6) +
      geom_jitter(width = 0.12, alpha = 0.35, size = 1.2) +
      labs(
        title = paste0(panel_label, ": ", var_name, " (", pretty_test(test_name), ")"),
        x = "Intervention",
        y = var_name
      ) +
      theme_minimal(base_size = 11)
  } else {
    dat$value <- as.factor(dat$value)
    ggplot(dat, aes(x = group, fill = value)) +
      geom_bar(position = "fill") +
      labs(
        title = paste0(panel_label, ": ", var_name, " (", pretty_test(test_name), ")"),
        x = "Intervention",
        y = "Proportion",
        fill = var_name
      ) +
      theme_minimal(base_size = 11)
  }
}

3.3 Symbol key

\(E\): exposure
\(Y\): outcome
\(C_k^{(6)}\): visit-6 covariate \(k\)
\(C_k^{(7)}\): visit-7 covariate \(k\)
\(B_k\): baseline covariate \(k\)
\(i\): participant index
\(t\): repeated-measure time index



3.4 Causal roles

Confounder: common cause of E and Y; adjust in primary models.
Mediator: on path E -> mediator -> Y; avoid in primary confounder set.
Collider: common effect; do not condition on it.

3.8 Collinearity






Understanding Collinearity & VIF




Pairwise Screen: A Spearman correlation, Epsilon-squared, or Cramer’s V \(\ge 0.70\) highlights redundant covariate pairs. In such cases, standard errors of the exposure effect estimate may inflate if both are kept.
Model-Matrix VIF: Variance Inflation Factors (VIF) summarize design-wide multicollinearity. Values below 5 indicate no concern. Values above 5 (or rank deficiency indicating exact aliasing) require reviewing the models for redundant parameters or category sparseness.



Collinearity is checked before interpreting expanded adjustment sets:

Numeric covariate pairs are screened with Spearman correlations within each visit-specific analytic panel.
Categorical covariate pairs are screened with Cramer’s V.
Mixed continuous-categorical pairs are screened with Epsilon-squared from Kruskal-Wallis tests.
Pairs with absolute values at or above 0.70 are flagged for review.



Show code
cramers_v <- function(x, y) {
  keep <- !is.na(x) & !is.na(y)
  x <- as.factor(x[keep])
  y <- as.factor(y[keep])
  if (length(x) == 0 || nlevels(x) < 2L || nlevels(y) < 2L) return(NA_real_)
  tab <- table(x, y)
  chi <- suppressWarnings(chisq.test(tab, correct = FALSE))
  n <- sum(tab)
  denom <- n * (min(dim(tab)) - 1)
  if (!is.finite(denom) || denom <= 0) return(NA_real_)
  sqrt(as.numeric(chi$statistic) / denom)
}

kruskal_epsilon_sq <- function(statistic, n, groups) {
  if (!is.finite(statistic) || !is.finite(n) || !is.finite(groups) || n <= groups) return(NA_real_)
  max(0, as.numeric((statistic - groups + 1) / (n - groups)))
}

collinearity_screen <- function(df, vars, panel_name, threshold = 0.70) {
  vars <- intersect(vars, names(df))
  vars <- vars[vapply(vars, function(v) sum(!is.na(df[[v]])) >= 10, logical(1))]
  if (length(vars) < 2L) return(data.frame())

  is_continuous <- function(val) {
    if (is.character(val) || is.factor(val) || is.logical(val) || inherits(val, "haven_labelled")) return(FALSE)
    val_num <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(val))
    sum(is.finite(val_num)) >= 8 && length(unique(val_num[is.finite(val_num)])) >= 6
  }

  pairs <- utils::combn(vars, 2, simplify = FALSE)
  bind_rows(lapply(pairs, function(pair) {
    x <- df[[pair[[1]]]]
    y <- df[[pair[[2]]]]
    
    keep <- !is.na(x) & !is.na(y)
    n_complete <- sum(keep)
    if (n_complete < 10) return(NULL)
    
    x_cont <- is_continuous(x)
    y_cont <- is_continuous(y)
    
    if (x_cont && y_cont) {
      value <- suppressWarnings(cor(as.numeric(x), as.numeric(y), use = "pairwise.complete.obs", method = "spearman"))
      metric <- "spearman_rho"
      flag <- is.finite(value) && abs(value) >= threshold
    } else if (!x_cont && !y_cont) {
      value <- cramers_v(x, y)
      metric <- "cramers_v"
      flag <- is.finite(value) && value >= threshold
    } else {
      # Mixed continuous-categorical pair
      if (x_cont) {
        continuous_val <- as.numeric(x)
        categorical_val <- factor(as.character(y))
      } else {
        continuous_val <- as.numeric(y)
        categorical_val <- factor(as.character(x))
      }
      
      if (nlevels(categorical_val) < 2) return(NULL)
      tst <- suppressWarnings(kruskal.test(continuous_val ~ categorical_val))
      value <- kruskal_epsilon_sq(tst$statistic, n_complete, nlevels(categorical_val))
      metric <- "epsilon_sq"
      flag <- is.finite(value) && value >= threshold
    }

    data.frame(
      panel = panel_name,
      covariate_1 = pair[[1]],
      covariate_2 = pair[[2]],
      metric = metric,
      value = value,
      flagged = flag,
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
  })) %>%
    filter(is.finite(value)) %>%
    arrange(desc(abs(value)))
}

3.9 Model-matrix collinearity
Model-matrix collinearity is checked before interpreting expanded adjustment sets. The fixed-effect design matrix is evaluated and the maximum design-column VIF is reported within each term; rank-deficient models indicate exact collinearity or aliasing.
Each row summarizes one model term within one analysis panel. N fits is the number of exposure-specific design matrices checked. Median VIF and Max VIF summarize the term-level VIF across those fits. VIF > 5 and VIF > 10 are counts of fits above those thresholds. Rank-deficient fits is the count of design matrices with exact rank deficiency.
Interpretation: VIF values below 5 indicate no material collinearity concern by the applied threshold. Terms with VIF above 5, especially above 10, or any rank-deficient fits should be reviewed for redundant covariates, sparse categories, or exact aliasing.


Show code

  "C_DietQuality_classes_6", "C_DietQuality_classes_7"
)

prep_model_data <- function(dat, vars) {
  vars <- intersect(vars, names(dat))
  out <- dat[, vars, drop = FALSE]
  for (nm in names(out)) {
    if (nm %in% categorical_vars || is.character(out[[nm]]) || is.factor(out[[nm]])) {
      out[[nm]] <- factor(as.character(out[[nm]]))
    } else {
      out[[nm]] <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(out[[nm]]))
    }
  }
  out
}

design_vif <- function(dat, exposure, covars, label) {
  rhs <- intersect(c(exposure, covars), names(dat))
  if (length(rhs) < 2 || !exposure %in% rhs) {
    return(tibble(model_family = label, exposure = exposure, term = NA_character_, n = 0L, matrix_rank = NA_integer_, matrix_cols = NA_integer_, rank_deficient = NA, max_column_vif = NA_real_))
  }

  dd <- prep_model_data(dat, rhs)
  dd <- dd[stats::complete.cases(dd), , drop = FALSE]
  if (nrow(dd) < 30) {
    return(tibble(model_family = label, exposure = exposure, term = NA_character_, n = nrow(dd), matrix_rank = NA_integer_, matrix_cols = NA_integer_, rank_deficient = NA, max_column_vif = NA_real_))
  }

  X <- tryCatch(model.matrix(reformulate(rhs), data = dd), error = function(e) NULL)
  if (is.null(X)) {
    return(tibble(model_family = label, exposure = exposure, term = NA_character_, n = nrow(dd), matrix_rank = NA_integer_, matrix_cols = NA_integer_, rank_deficient = NA, max_column_vif = NA_real_))
  }

  assign_vec <- attr(X, "assign")
  term_labels <- attr(terms(reformulate(rhs)), "term.labels")
  X <- X[, colnames(X) != "(Intercept)", drop = FALSE]
  assign_vec <- assign_vec[colnames(model.matrix(reformulate(rhs), data = dd)) != "(Intercept)"]
  keep <- apply(X, 2, function(z) stats::sd(z, na.rm = TRUE) > 0)
  X <- X[, keep, drop = FALSE]
  assign_vec <- assign_vec[keep]

  rank_x <- qr(X)$rank
  p_x <- ncol(X)
  if (p_x < 2) {
    vif_vals <- rep(NA_real_, p_x)
  } else {
    vif_vals <- vapply(seq_len(p_x), function(j) {
      xj <- X[, j]
      others <- X[, -j, drop = FALSE]
      r2 <- tryCatch(summary(lm(xj ~ others))$r.squared, error = function(e) NA_real_)
      if (!is.finite(r2) || r2 >= 1) return(Inf)
      1 / (1 - r2)
    }, numeric(1))
  }

  tibble(
    model_family = label,
    exposure = exposure,
    term = term_labels[assign_vec],
    n = nrow(dd),
    matrix_rank = rank_x,
    matrix_cols = p_x,
    rank_deficient = rank_x < p_x,
    column_vif = vif_vals
  ) %>%
    group_by(model_family, exposure, term, n, matrix_rank, matrix_cols, rank_deficient) %>%
    summarise(max_column_vif = max(column_vif, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")
}

3.10 Exposure-covariate screening






Purposeful Selection Screening



Purposeful-style screening checks whether candidate variables show empirical associations with the exposures and/or outcomes, which can support sensitivity-adjustment decisions but does not establish confounding on its own. * A significance threshold of \(p < 0.20\) is typically used to screen potential candidates rather than the standard \(p < 0.05\) to avoid omitting weak confounders. * Small \(q\)-values (BH adjusted) indicate associations that survive multiple-testing adjustments.


This table screens covariate associations with diet exposures. Within each visit panel, each covariate is tested against each relevant diet exposure, then summarized as one row per covariate.
Tests by variable type:

Spearman for continuous exposure and continuous covariate pairs.
Kruskal-Wallis for continuous exposure vs categorical covariate, and categorical exposure vs continuous covariate.
Fisher exact for categorical exposure and categorical covariate pairs.

Tests is the number of exposure-covariate tests in the row. Min p is the smallest raw p-value across those tests. Min q is the smallest BH-adjusted p-value within the visit panel. q < 0.10 is the number of exposure-covariate tests for that covariate with q < 0.10.
Interpretation: a covariate with q < 0.10 greater than zero is associated with at least one diet exposure after BH correction at the 10% FDR level. A value of zero means no exposure-covariate association met that threshold in the panel.

var_kind <- function(x, nm = NULL) {
  if (!is.null(nm) && nm %in% categorical_vars) return("categorical")
  if (is.character(x) || is.factor(x)) return("categorical")
  x_num <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(x))
  if (sum(is.finite(x_num)) < 8) return("categorical")
  if (length(unique(x_num[is.finite(x_num)])) < 6) return("categorical")
  "continuous"
}

assoc_test <- function(dat, exposure, covar, family) {
  if (!all(c(exposure, covar) %in% names(dat))) {
    return(tibble(model_family = family, exposure = exposure, covariate = covar, test = "missing", n = 0L, statistic = NA_real_, p.value = NA_real_))
  }
  dd <- dat[, c(exposure, covar), drop = FALSE]
  dd <- dd[stats::complete.cases(dd), , drop = FALSE]
  if (nrow(dd) < 20) {
    return(tibble(model_family = family, exposure = exposure, covariate = covar, test = "too_few_complete", n = nrow(dd), statistic = NA_real_, p.value = NA_real_))
  }
  x_kind <- var_kind(dd[[exposure]], exposure)
  z_kind <- var_kind(dd[[covar]], covar)
  x_num <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(dd[[exposure]]))
  z_num <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(dd[[covar]]))

  if (x_kind == "continuous" && z_kind == "continuous") {
    tst <- suppressWarnings(cor.test(x_num, z_num, method = "spearman", exact = FALSE))
    return(tibble(model_family = family, exposure = exposure, covariate = covar, test = "Spearman", n = nrow(dd), statistic = unname(tst$estimate), p.value = tst$p.value))
  }

  if (x_kind == "continuous" && z_kind == "categorical") {
    g <- factor(as.character(dd[[covar]]))
    if (nlevels(g) < 2) return(tibble(model_family = family, exposure = exposure, covariate = covar, test = "constant", n = nrow(dd), statistic = NA_real_, p.value = NA_real_))
    tst <- suppressWarnings(kruskal.test(x_num ~ g))
    return(tibble(model_family = family, exposure = exposure, covariate = covar, test = "Kruskal-Wallis", n = nrow(dd), statistic = unname(tst$statistic), p.value = tst$p.value))
  }

  if (x_kind == "categorical" && z_kind == "continuous") {
    g <- factor(as.character(dd[[exposure]]))
    if (nlevels(g) < 2) return(tibble(model_family = family, exposure = exposure, covariate = covar, test = "constant", n = nrow(dd), statistic = NA_real_, p.value = NA_real_))
    tst <- suppressWarnings(kruskal.test(z_num ~ g))
    return(tibble(model_family = family, exposure = exposure, covariate = covar, test = "Kruskal-Wallis", n = nrow(dd), statistic = unname(tst$statistic), p.value = tst$p.value))
  }

  tab <- table(factor(as.character(dd[[exposure]])), factor(as.character(dd[[covar]])))
  if (any(dim(tab) < 2)) return(tibble(model_family = family, exposure = exposure, covariate = covar, test = "constant", n = nrow(dd), statistic = NA_real_, p.value = NA_real_))
  tst <- suppressWarnings(fisher.test(tab))
  tibble(model_family = family, exposure = exposure, covariate = covar, test = "Fisher exact", n = nrow(dd), statistic = NA_real_, p.value = tst$p.value)
}

3.11 Covariate-outcome screening
This table screens covariate associations with metabolite outcome structure. Principal component analysis is run on the visit-specific metabolite matrix, and each covariate is tested against the first five metabolite principal components.

The first five metabolite principal components are calculated separately within visit 6 and visit 7.
Continuous covariates are screened against each PC with Spearman correlation.
Categorical covariates are screened with Kruskal-Wallis tests.
BH correction is applied within visit-specific PC screen.

Each row summarizes one covariate within one visit-specific metabolite principal component panel. Tests is the number of principal component tests in the row, with a maximum of five. Min p is the smallest raw p-value across those tests. Min q is the smallest BH-adjusted p-value within the visit panel. q < 0.10 is the number of covariate-principal component tests with q < 0.10.
Interpretation: a covariate with q < 0.10 greater than zero is associated with at least one of the first five metabolite principal components after BH correction at the 10% FDR level. This indicates association with broad metabolite variation, not with a specific metabolite.

make_pc_scores <- function(dat, features, prefix) {
  features <- intersect(features, names(dat))
  if (length(features) == 0) return(dat %>% select(StudyID))
  mat <- as.matrix(dat[, features, drop = FALSE])
  mat <- apply(mat, 2, function(x) suppressWarnings(as.numeric(x)))
  if (is.null(dim(mat))) mat <- matrix(mat, ncol = 1L)
  keep <- colMeans(!is.na(mat)) >= 0.7 & apply(mat, 2, stats::sd, na.rm = TRUE) > 0
  mat <- mat[, keep, drop = FALSE]
  if (ncol(mat) == 0) return(dat %>% select(StudyID))
  for (j in seq_len(ncol(mat))) {
    mat[is.na(mat[, j]), j] <- stats::median(mat[, j], na.rm = TRUE)
  }
  pcs <- stats::prcomp(mat, center = TRUE, scale. = TRUE)
  scores <- as.data.frame(pcs$x[, seq_len(min(5L, ncol(pcs$x))), drop = FALSE])
  names(scores) <- paste0(prefix, "_PC", seq_along(scores))
  bind_cols(dat %>% select(StudyID), scores)
}

pc_assoc <- function(dat, covar, pc, family) {
  if (!all(c(covar, pc) %in% names(dat))) {
    return(tibble(model_family = family, covariate = covar, outcome_summary = pc, test = "missing", n = 0L, p.value = NA_real_))
  }
  dd <- dat[, c(covar, pc), drop = FALSE]
  dd <- dd[stats::complete.cases(dd), , drop = FALSE]
  if (nrow(dd) < 20) {
    return(tibble(model_family = family, covariate = covar, outcome_summary = pc, test = "too_few_complete", n = nrow(dd), p.value = NA_real_))
  }

  y <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(dd[[pc]]))
  if (var_kind(dd[[covar]], covar) == "continuous") {
    x <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(dd[[covar]]))
    tst <- suppressWarnings(stats::cor.test(x, y, method = "spearman", exact = FALSE))
    return(tibble(model_family = family, covariate = covar, outcome_summary = pc, test = "Spearman", n = nrow(dd), p.value = tst$p.value))
  }

  g <- factor(as.character(dd[[covar]]))
  if (nlevels(g) < 2) {
    return(tibble(model_family = family, covariate = covar, outcome_summary = pc, test = "constant", n = nrow(dd), p.value = NA_real_))
  }
  tst <- suppressWarnings(stats::kruskal.test(y ~ g))
  tibble(model_family = family, covariate = covar, outcome_summary = pc, test = "Kruskal-Wallis", n = nrow(dd), p.value = tst$p.value)
}

3.13 Covariate decision support (reference models)
Reference-model beta-change and complete-case impact are provided to support retain/drop discussion.


Show code
beta_change_ref <- function(df, exposure, covars, candidate, outcome_pc) {
  need <- unique(c(outcome_pc, exposure, covars))
  need <- need[need %in% names(df)]
  if (!all(c(outcome_pc, exposure, candidate) %in% need)) return(c(NA_real_, NA_integer_, NA_integer_))
  d <- df[, need, drop = FALSE]
  d <- d[stats::complete.cases(d), , drop = FALSE]
  if (nrow(d) < 30) return(c(NA_real_, nrow(d), NA_integer_))

  cov_full <- covars[covars %in% names(d)]
  if (!candidate %in% cov_full) return(c(NA_real_, nrow(d), NA_integer_))
  cov_red <- setdiff(cov_full, candidate)
  f_full <- as.formula(paste(outcome_pc, "~", paste(c(exposure, cov_full), collapse = " + ")))
  f_red <- as.formula(paste(outcome_pc, "~", paste(c(exposure, cov_red), collapse = " + ")))
  m_full <- tryCatch(lm(f_full, data = d), error = function(e) NULL)
  m_red <- tryCatch(lm(f_red, data = d), error = function(e) NULL)
  if (is.null(m_full) || is.null(m_red)) return(c(NA_real_, nrow(d), NA_integer_))
  b_full <- coef(m_full)[exposure]
  b_red <- coef(m_red)[exposure]
  if (is.na(b_full) || is.na(b_red) || !is.finite(b_full) || abs(b_full) < 1e-8) return(c(NA_real_, nrow(model.frame(m_full)), nrow(model.frame(m_red))))
  c(abs((b_red - b_full) / b_full) * 100, nrow(model.frame(m_full)), nrow(model.frame(m_red)))
}

    data.frame(

      Covariate = cov,

      n_full = as.integer(stats_vec[2]),
      n_drop_covariate = as.integer(stats_vec[3]),
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE

  }),
  lapply(unique(c(core_covars_v7_lean, sensitivity_covars)), function(cov) {

    data.frame(

      n_full = as.integer(stats_vec[2]),
      n_drop_covariate = as.integer(stats_vec[3]),
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
  })
) %>%
  left_join(
    combined_screen %>% transmute(Panel, Covariate, `Any p < 0.20` = ifelse(`Exposure p < 0.20` > 0 | `Outcome p < 0.20` > 0, "yes", "no")),
    by = c("Panel", "Covariate")

  mutate(
    Role = case_when(

      `Any p < 0.20` == "yes" | `Retain signal (>10% beta change)` == "yes" ~ "keep_sensitivity",
      TRUE ~ "consider_drop"
    ),
    `Ref beta change %` = round(beta_change_pct, 1),
    `N full` = n_full,
    `N drop covariate` = n_drop_covariate
  ) %>%
  arrange(Panel, Covariate)

  for (pnl in unique(keep_sens$Panel)) {

    cat(sprintf("  - *%s*: %s\n", pnl, paste(vars_pnl, collapse = ", ")))

} else {

}


Sensitivity Adjusters (Carried Forward): The following sensitivity covariates show screening signals (p < 0.20 or >10% change in exposure effect) and are carried forward in sensitivity analyses:

visit 6: CHealthy6, CMacrosomy, CSGA, MPRSmoke, Premature

Show code
if (nrow(drop_candidates) > 0) {
  cat("- **Candidates for Omission**: The following sensitivity covariates did not meet the screening signal thresholds (p < 0.20 or >10% reference beta change). They are flagged for possible omission to simplify the models, prevent over-specification, and maximize complete-case sample size:\n")
  for (pnl in unique(drop_candidates$Panel)) {
    vars_pnl <- drop_candidates$Covariate[drop_candidates$Panel == pnl]
    
    # Calculate potential sample size recovery
    pnl_df <- decision_tbl %>% filter(Panel == pnl, Covariate %in% vars_pnl)
    n_recovered <- max(pnl_df$`N drop covariate` - pnl_df$`N full`, na.rm = TRUE)
    
    cat(sprintf("  - *%s*: %s (Omitting these recovers up to **%d** complete cases)\n", 
                pnl, paste(vars_pnl, collapse = ", "), if(is.finite(n_recovered) && n_recovered > 0) as.integer(n_recovered) else 0L))
  }
} else {
  cat("- **Candidates for Omission**: No sensitivity covariates met the automated omission criteria under the default signal thresholds. However, to prevent model over-specification (overfitting) and preserve degrees of freedom, you should still evaluate whether retaining all sensitivity adjusters is justified by the clinical context. If they do not materially alter the exposure estimates, sticking to the minimal core DAG set is recommended.\n")
}

3.16 Missingness by visit
Visit-specific missingness is reported as a data-quality diagnostic and to support interpretation of precision and sensitivity analyses.


Show code
missing_table <- function(df, vars, idx) {
  vars <- vars[vars %in% names(df)]
  if (length(vars) == 0) {
    return(data.frame(
      Covariate = character(),
      Available_N = integer(),
      Missing_pct = numeric()
    ))
  }
  if (length(idx) == 0) {
    return(data.frame(
      Note = "No rows available for this visit-specific analytic panel; missingness was not calculated.",
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    ))
  }
  data.frame(
    Covariate = vars,
    Available_N = sapply(vars, function(v) sum(!is.na(df[[v]][idx]))),
    Missing_pct = sapply(vars, function(v) round(mean(is.na(df[[v]][idx])) * 100, 1))
  ) %>% arrange(desc(Missing_pct))
}

title: Covariate and model specification
text:
4 Model specification

4.1 Proposed models

Visit-specific linear models:

Y_v6 ~ E_v6 + core_covars_v6
Y_v7 ~ E_v7 + core_covars_v7_lean

Longitudinal conditional model:

Y_v7 ~ E_v6 + Y_v6 + baseline_covariates
this model is fit separately for each metabolite pair so that visit-7 metabolite values are adjusted for the same metabolite measured at visit 6

Repeated-measures mixed model:

Y_it ~ E_it + visit_t + E_it:visit_t + repeated_core_covariates + (1|child)
E_it is the visit-harmonized child diet exposure, allowing the same dietary construct to be evaluated across visits 6 and 7
sensitivity models additionally harmonize the healthy-diet indicator




4.2 Mathematical model forms

Visit-specific models:

\[
Y_{i,6} = \beta_0 + \beta_1 E_{i,6} + \sum_{k=1}^{p} \gamma_k C^{(6)}_{i,k} + \varepsilon_{i,6}
\]
\[
Y_{i,7} = \beta_0 + \beta_1 E_{i,7} + \sum_{k=1}^{q} \delta_k C^{(7)}_{i,k} + \varepsilon_{i,7}
\]

Longitudinal conditional model:

\[
Y_{i,7} = \beta_0 + \beta_1 E_{i,6} + \beta_2 Y_{i,6} + \sum_{k=1}^{r} \eta_k B_{i,k} + \varepsilon_i
\]

Repeated-measures mixed model (child i, time t):

\[
Y_{i,t} = \beta_0 + \beta_1 E_{i,t} + \beta_2 t + \beta_3(E_{i,t} \times t) + \sum_{k=1}^{s} \theta_k R_{i,t,k} + u_i + \varepsilon_{i,t}
\]
with random intercept:
\[
u_i \sim \mathcal{N}(0, \sigma_u^2), \quad \varepsilon_{i,t} \sim \mathcal{N}(0, \sigma^2)
\]

build_variable_key <- function(df) {
  nm <- names(df)
  make_friendly <- function(x) {
    y <- gsub("([a-z])([A-Z])", "\\1 \\2", x)
    y <- gsub("([A-Za-z])([0-9])", "\\1 \\2", y)
    y <- gsub("([0-9])([A-Za-z])", "\\1 \\2", y)
    y <- gsub("[._]+", " ", y)
    y <- gsub("\\s+", " ", trimws(y))
    tools::toTitleCase(tolower(y))
  }
  var_label <- sapply(nm, function(v) {
    lb <- attr(df[[v]], "label")
    if (is.null(lb) || is.na(lb) || lb == "") "" else as.character(lb)
  })
  value_levels <- sapply(nm, function(v) {
    labs <- attr(df[[v]], "labels")
    if (is.null(labs) || length(labs) == 0) return("")
    paste(sprintf("%s = %s", names(labs), unname(labs)), collapse = "; ")
  })
  data.frame(
    Friendly_Name = sapply(nm, make_friendly),
    Variable_Code = nm,
    Variable_Label = var_label,
    Value_Levels = value_levels,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
}

)

title: Secondary figures: child diet and metabolites
text:
These figures show exposure-level detail, adjustment robustness, and cross-question pattern coherence for the linear-model components (Q1-Q3). They are not the primary inferential summaries and do not cover the warning-heavy repeated-measures Q4 analysis.
Selected exposures used in this page. These are rule-based appendix exemplars, not hand-picked headline results:
Show code
make_table(rep_tbl, caption = "Selected representative exposures for secondary figures")

title: Secondary figures: child diet and metabolites
text:
1 Figure A1. Global association screen


Show code
screen_df <- bind_rows(
  q1 %>% filter(exposure == rep_q1) %>% mutate(analysis = "Concurrent: 2-year diet and 2-year metabolites"),
  q2 %>% filter(exposure == rep_q2) %>% mutate(analysis = "Concurrent: 5-6-year diet and 5-6-year metabolites"),
  q3 %>% filter(exposure == rep_q3) %>% mutate(analysis = "Prospective: 2-year diet and 5-6-year metabolites")
) %>%
  add_feature_meta() %>%
  group_by(analysis) %>%
  arrange(p.value, .by_group = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(rank = row_number(), logp = safe_logp(p.value), sig = q.value < 0.1) %>%
  ungroup()

screen_df %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = rank, y = logp, color = feature_group)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7, size = 1.4) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = -log10(0.05), linetype = "dashed", color = "grey45") +
  facet_wrap(~ analysis, scales = "free_x") +
  labs(
    x = "Ordered metabolite rank",
    y = "-log10(p)",
    color = "Metabolite class",
    title = "Global association screens for selected exposures"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 16)








Figure 1: Global association screen (p-value ranking) for selected dietary exposures at 2 and 5-6 years. Points colored by metabolite class.

for (analysis_i in analysis_order) {
  panel_df <- forest_primary %>% filter(analysis == analysis_i)
  p <- panel_df %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = estimate, y = fct_reorder(feature_lab, estimate), color = feature_group)) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey45") +

      y = NULL,
      color = "Metabolite class",

    ) +

section: 3 Figure A3. Sensitivity and model comparison
text:
3 Figure A3. Sensitivity and model comparison


Show code
top_features_q1 <- q1 %>%
  filter(exposure == rep_q1) %>%
  slice_min(q.value, n = 10, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  pull(feature)

top_features_q2 <- q2 %>%
  filter(exposure == rep_q2) %>%
  slice_min(q.value, n = 10, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  pull(feature)

top_features_q3 <- q3 %>%
  filter(exposure == rep_q3) %>%
  slice_min(q.value, n = 10, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  pull(feature)

fit_cross_models <- function(df, exposure, features, model_sets, analysis_name) {
  bind_rows(lapply(features, function(ft) {
    bind_rows(lapply(names(model_sets), function(model_nm) {
      fit <- fit_lm_exposure(
        df = df,
        outcome = ft,
        exposure = exposure,
        covars = model_sets[[model_nm]]
      )
      if (is.null(fit)) return(NULL)
      fit %>%
        mutate(
          analysis = analysis_name,
          model = model_nm,
          feature = ft
        )
    }))
  }))
}

comp_q1 <- fit_cross_models(
  df = df6,
  exposure = rep_q1,
  features = top_features_q1,
  model_sets = list(
    "Minimally adjusted" = minimal_v6,
    "Primary adjusted" = core_v6,
    "Technical/acute-state adjusted" = technical_v6
  ),
  analysis_name = "Concurrent: 2-year diet and 2-year metabolites"
)

comp_q2 <- fit_cross_models(
  df = df7,
  exposure = rep_q2,
  features = top_features_q2,
  model_sets = list(
    "Minimally adjusted" = minimal_v7,
    "Primary adjusted" = core_v7,
    "Technical/acute-state adjusted" = technical_v7
  ),
  analysis_name = "Concurrent: 5-6-year diet and 5-6-year metabolites"
)

fit_prospective_models <- function(df6, df7, exposure, features, core_covars_v6) {
  bind_rows(lapply(features, function(ft) {
    if (!(ft %in% names(df6)) || !(ft %in% names(df7))) return(NULL)

    dat <- inner_join(
      df7 %>% select(StudyID, outcome = all_of(ft)),
      df6 %>% select(StudyID, exposure_value = all_of(exposure), all_of(core_covars_v6), baseline = all_of(ft)),
      by = "StudyID"
    )

    base_covars <- intersect(core_covars_v6, names(dat))
    bind_rows(
      fit_lm_exposure(dat, outcome = "outcome", exposure = "exposure_value", covars = character(0)) %>%
        mutate(model = "Unadjusted"),
      fit_lm_exposure(dat, outcome = "outcome", exposure = "exposure_value", covars = base_covars) %>%
        mutate(model = "Primary adjusted"),
      fit_lm_exposure(dat, outcome = "outcome", exposure = "exposure_value", covars = c(base_covars, "baseline")) %>%
        mutate(model = "Baseline-metabolite adjusted")
    ) %>%
      mutate(feature = ft)
  }))
}

comp_q3 <- fit_prospective_models(
  df6 = df6,
  df7 = df7,
  exposure = rep_q3,
  features = top_features_q3,
  core_covars_v6 = core_v6
) %>%
  mutate(analysis = "Prospective: 2-year diet and 5-6-year metabolites")

comp_all <- bind_rows(comp_q1, comp_q2, comp_q3) %>%
  add_feature_meta() %>%
  mutate(
    feature_lab = paste0(feature_group, " | ", feature),
    model = factor(
      model,
      levels = c(
        "Unadjusted",
        "Minimally adjusted",
        "Primary adjusted",
        "Technical/acute-state adjusted",
        "Baseline-metabolite adjusted"
      )
    )
  )

for (analysis_i in analysis_order) {
  panel_df <- comp_all %>% filter(analysis == analysis_i)
  if (nrow(panel_df) == 0) next

  p <- panel_df %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = feature_lab, y = estimate, color = model)) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey45") +
    geom_pointrange(
      aes(ymin = conf.low, ymax = conf.high),
      position = position_dodge(width = 0.7),
      fatten = 1.5,
      linewidth = 0.35
    ) +
    coord_flip() +
    labs(
      x = NULL,
      y = "Standardized beta (95% CI)",
      color = "Model",
      title = paste0("Sensitivity/model comparison: ", analysis_i)
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 16)
  print(p)
}

bases_q1 <- unique(strip_visit_suffix(q1$exposure))
bases_q2 <- unique(strip_visit_suffix(q2$exposure))
bases_q3 <- unique(strip_visit_suffix(q3$exposure))
common_bases <- intersect(intersect(bases_q1, bases_q2), bases_q3)

base_score <- bind_rows(
  q1 %>% mutate(base = strip_visit_suffix(exposure)),
  q2 %>% mutate(base = strip_visit_suffix(exposure)),
  q3 %>% mutate(base = strip_visit_suffix(exposure))
) %>%
  filter(base %in% common_bases) %>%
  group_by(base) %>%
  summarise(best_q = min(q.value, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(best_q)

selected_base <- if (nrow(base_score) > 0) base_score$base[[1]] else NA_character_

exp_q1 <- q1 %>% filter(strip_visit_suffix(exposure) == selected_base) %>% distinct(exposure) %>% slice_head(n = 1) %>% pull(exposure)
exp_q2 <- q2 %>% filter(strip_visit_suffix(exposure) == selected_base) %>% distinct(exposure) %>% slice_head(n = 1) %>% pull(exposure)
exp_q3 <- q3 %>% filter(strip_visit_suffix(exposure) == selected_base) %>% distinct(exposure) %>% slice_head(n = 1) %>% pull(exposure)

comp_age <- bind_rows(
  q1 %>% filter(exposure == exp_q1) %>% mutate(model = "Concurrent at 2 years"),
  q2 %>% filter(exposure == exp_q2) %>% mutate(model = "Concurrent at 5-6 years"),
  q3 %>% filter(exposure == exp_q3) %>% mutate(model = "Prospective 2-year to 5-6-year")
) %>%
  add_feature_meta()

top_comp_features <- comp_age %>%
  group_by(feature) %>%
  summarise(best_q = min(q.value, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  slice_min(best_q, n = 20, with_ties = FALSE)

comp_age_plot <- comp_age %>%
  semi_join(top_comp_features, by = "feature") %>%
  mutate(
    feature_lab = paste0(feature_group, " | ", feature),
    model = factor(
      model,
      levels = c("Concurrent at 2 years", "Concurrent at 5-6 years", "Prospective 2-year to 5-6-year")
    )
  )

comp_age_plot %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = feature_lab, y = estimate, color = model)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey45") +
  geom_pointrange(
    aes(ymin = conf.low, ymax = conf.high),
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.7),
    fatten = 1.4,
    linewidth = 0.35
  ) +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = "Standardized beta (95% CI)",
    color = "Analysis frame",
    title = paste0("Comparative summary across ages for exposure family: ", selected_base)
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 16)








Figure 8: Comparative effect sizes across the two concurrent models and the prospective model for one matched exposure family selected by the best observed q-value across Q1-Q3.

title: Primary results: child diet and metabolites
text: Child diet is related to child serum metabolites at 2 years, 5-6 years, and across the repeated 2-year to 5-6-year follow-up window. The repeated-measures component should be interpreted more cautiously than the linear-model components. For multi-level diet-quality class exposures in the linear-model tables below, the reported P and Q values come from omnibus factor tests across class levels, so no single beta or confidence interval is shown for those rows.

5 Repeated-measures associations across 2 years and 5-6 years
The repeated-measures model uses the paired visit-6 and visit-7 diet constructs within child and reports both the pooled diet main effect and the diet-by-visit interaction.
These estimates should be treated more cautiously than Q1-Q3. In the executed analysis, this model family produced singular-fit and rank-deficiency warnings. The saved results object does not currently encode warning counts, so the tables below report coefficient output, not clean-fit certification.
Diet main-effect rows. Complete-case n across fitted models: 199-335.

8 Sensitivity summary


Show code
comp_q1 <- compare_primary_sensitivity(q1, q1_sens, c("exposure", "feature", "visit")) %>% mutate(analysis = "Q1 concurrent")
comp_q2 <- compare_primary_sensitivity(q2, q2_sens, c("exposure", "feature", "visit")) %>% mutate(analysis = "Q2 concurrent")
comp_q3 <- compare_primary_sensitivity(q3, q3_sens, c("exposure", "feature", "visit_exposure", "visit_outcome")) %>% mutate(analysis = "Q3 prospective")
comp_q4 <- compare_primary_sensitivity(q4, q4_sens, c("exposure", "feature", "term")) %>% mutate(analysis = "Q4 repeated")

comp_all <- bind_rows(comp_q1, comp_q2, comp_q3, comp_q4)

if (nrow(comp_all) > 0) {
  summary_tbl <- comp_all %>%
    mutate(exposure_family = exposure_family(exposure)) %>%
    group_by(analysis, exposure_family, exposure) %>%
    summarise(
      pairs_compared = n(),
      median_abs_delta = median(abs_delta, na.rm = TRUE),
      p90_abs_delta = as.numeric(quantile(abs_delta, 0.90, na.rm = TRUE, names = FALSE)),
      direction_flip_fraction = mean(direction_flip, na.rm = TRUE),
      q_reclassification_fraction = mean(q_reclassified, na.rm = TRUE),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    mutate(
      median_abs_delta = round(median_abs_delta, 3),
      p90_abs_delta = round(p90_abs_delta, 3),
      direction_flip_fraction = round(direction_flip_fraction, 3),
      q_reclassification_fraction = round(q_reclassification_fraction, 3)
    )

  datatable(
    summary_tbl,
    caption = "Primary-vs-sensitivity estimate stability",
    filter = "top",
    options = list(pageLength = 10, scrollX = FALSE, autoWidth = FALSE)
  )
}

prospective associations from 2-year diet to 5-6-year metabolites, conditioning on the same metabolite at 2 years
repeated-measures associations across 2 years and 5-6 years

title: Study overview
text:
6 Multiple testing
False-discovery control is based on Benjamini-Hochberg adjustment across metabolites, but the adjustment scope differs by model family.

Concurrent outputs apply BH within each exposure-specific result set.
Prospective outputs apply BH within each exposure-specific result set.
Repeated-measures outputs apply BH separately within each exposure and coefficient family.

title: Study overview
text:
7 Result inventory


Show code
q4_main <- results$q4 %>% filter(!grepl(":visit_num$", term))
q4_interaction <- results$q4 %>% filter(grepl(":visit_num$", term))

inventory_tbl <- bind_rows(
  add_exposure_family(results$q1) %>% mutate(component = "Concurrent at 2 Years", n_col = n),
  add_exposure_family(results$q2) %>% mutate(component = "Concurrent at 5-6 Years", n_col = n),
  add_exposure_family(results$q3) %>% mutate(component = "Prospective: 2-Year Diet to 5-6-Year Metabolites", n_col = n),
  add_exposure_family(q4_main) %>% mutate(component = "Repeated-Measures Main Effect", n_col = n_obs),
  add_exposure_family(q4_interaction) %>% mutate(component = "Repeated-Measures Diet-by-Time Interaction", n_col = n_obs)
) %>%
  group_by(component, exposure_family) %>%
  summarise(
    result_rows = n(),
    significant_rows_q_lt_0_10 = sum(q.value < 0.10, na.rm = TRUE),
    complete_case_n_range = {
      vals <- sort(unique(n_col[is.finite(n_col)]))
      if (length(vals) == 0) NA_character_ else paste0(min(vals), "-", max(vals))
    },
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(exposure_family = factor(exposure_family, levels = family_order)) %>%
  arrange(component, exposure_family)

make_table(inventory_tbl, caption = "Returned result inventory", table_mode = "compact")

title: Study overview
text:
8 Execution status


Show code
status_tbl <- model_status %>%
  mutate(
    question = dplyr::case_when(
      question == "Q1" ~ "Concurrent at 2 Years",
      question == "Q2" ~ "Concurrent at 5-6 Years",
      question == "Q3" ~ "Prospective",
      question == "Q4" ~ "Repeated-Measures",
      TRUE ~ question
    ),
    exposure = pretty_var(exposure),
    n_complete = ifelse(
      is.na(n_complete_min) | !is.finite(n_complete_min),
      NA_character_,
      ifelse(n_complete_min == n_complete_max, as.character(n_complete_min), paste0(n_complete_min, "-", n_complete_max))
    )
  ) %>%
  select(question, analysis, exposure, status, reason, n_complete, n_features_tested, term_family)

make_table(status_tbl, caption = "Saved execution-status inventory", table_mode = "wide")

title: Study overview
text:
9 Interpretation notes

The concurrent and prospective models carry the clearest inferential weight.
The prospective model is conditional on the earlier metabolite value, so it should be interpreted as a follow-up association rather than a pure baseline-only prediction.
The repeated-measures component is included because it is part of the Study 1 question set, but it remains the most warning-prone analysis family and should be interpreted more cautiously than the linear components.
Detailed coefficient tables, sensitivity comparisons, and supporting figures are reported elsewhere in the site; this page is the study-level map.

2 Supplementary Table S2. Exposure definitions

  tibble(
    component = "Concurrent at 2 Years",
    exposure_timing = "2-year child diet",
    exposure_code = q1_expos
  ),

    transmute(
      component = "Repeated-Measures Models",

  sensitivity_additions = c(
    "Small-for-gestational-age; macrosomy; healthy-diet score at 2 years",
    "Maternal smoking; prematurity; small-for-gestational-age; macrosomy; healthy-diet score at 5-6 years",
    "Primary prospective block plus small-for-gestational-age; macrosomy; healthy-diet score at 2 years",
    "Primary repeated-measures block plus small-for-gestational-age; macrosomy; time-varying healthy-diet score"
  ),
  multiple_testing = c(
    "Benjamini-Hochberg across metabolites within each exposure-specific concurrent model",
    "Benjamini-Hochberg across metabolites within each exposure-specific concurrent model",
    "Benjamini-Hochberg across metabolites within each exposure-specific prospective model",
    "Benjamini-Hochberg across metabolites applied separately within each repeated-measures exposure and coefficient family"
  )
)

make_table(model_tbl, caption = "Supplementary Table S3. Primary model specifications")

title: Supplementary tables
section: 4 Supplementary Table S4. Returned result inventory
text:
4 Supplementary Table S4. Returned result inventory


Show code
q4_main <- q4 %>% filter(!grepl(":", term))
q4_interaction <- q4 %>% filter(grepl(":", term))

inventory_tbl <- bind_rows(
  add_exposure_family(q1) %>% mutate(component = "Concurrent at 2 Years", n_col = n),
  add_exposure_family(q2) %>% mutate(component = "Concurrent at 5-6 Years", n_col = n),
  add_exposure_family(q3) %>% mutate(component = "Prospective: 2-Year Diet to 5-6-Year Metabolites", n_col = n),
  add_exposure_family(q4_main) %>% mutate(component = "Repeated-Measures Main Effect", n_col = n_obs),
  add_exposure_family(q4_interaction) %>% mutate(component = "Repeated-Measures Diet-by-Time Interaction", n_col = n_obs)
) %>%
  group_by(component, exposure_family) %>%
  summarise(
    result_rows = n(),
    significant_rows_q_lt_0_10 = sum(q.value < 0.10, na.rm = TRUE),
    complete_case_n_range = {
      vals <- sort(unique(n_col[is.finite(n_col)]))
      if (length(vals) == 0) NA_character_ else paste0(min(vals), "-", max(vals))
    },
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(exposure_family = factor(exposure_family, levels = family_order)) %>%
  arrange(component, exposure_family)

make_table(inventory_tbl, caption = "Supplementary Table S4. Returned result inventory")

title: Supplementary tables
section: 5 Supplementary Table S5. Candidate associations with q < 0.10
text:
5 Supplementary Table S5. Candidate associations with q < 0.10


Show code
candidate_linear <- bind_rows(
  q1 %>% mutate(component = "Concurrent at 2 Years"),
  q2 %>% mutate(component = "Concurrent at 5-6 Years"),
  q3 %>% mutate(component = "Prospective: 2-Year Diet to 5-6-Year Metabolites")
) %>%
  filter(q.value < 0.10) %>%
  mutate(multilevel_factor = grepl("\\|", term)) %>%
  transmute(
    component,
    exposure_family = exposure_family(exposure),
    exposure = pretty_var(exposure),
    feature,
    term = if_else(multilevel_factor, "Omnibus multi-level factor test", NA_character_),
    n,
    beta = if_else(multilevel_factor, NA_real_, estimate),
    conf_low = if_else(multilevel_factor, NA_real_, conf.low),
    conf_high = if_else(multilevel_factor, NA_real_, conf.high),
    p_value = p.value,
    q_value = q.value
  )

candidate_q4 <- bind_rows(
  q4_main %>% mutate(component = "Repeated-Measures Main Effect"),
  q4_interaction %>% mutate(component = "Repeated-Measures Diet-by-Time Interaction")
) %>%
  filter(q.value < 0.10) %>%
  transmute(
    component,
    exposure_family = exposure_family(exposure),
    exposure = pretty_var(exposure),
    feature,
    term,
    n_obs,
    n_ind,
    beta = estimate,
    conf_low = estimate - 1.96 * std.error,
    conf_high = estimate + 1.96 * std.error,
    p_value = p.value,
    q_value = q.value
  )

candidate_tbl <- bind_rows(
  candidate_linear %>% mutate(n_obs = n, n_ind = NA_real_),
  candidate_q4 %>% rename(n = n_obs)
) %>%
  mutate(exposure_family = factor(exposure_family, levels = family_order)) %>%
  arrange(exposure_family, q_value, p_value)

make_table(candidate_tbl, caption = "Supplementary Table S5. Candidate associations meeting q < 0.10")

title: Supplementary tables
section: 6 Supplementary Table S6. Primary-vs-sensitivity stability summary
text:
6 Supplementary Table S6. Primary-vs-sensitivity stability summary


Show code
stability_tbl <- bind_rows(
  compare_primary_sensitivity(q1, q1_sens, c("exposure", "feature", "visit")) %>% mutate(component = "Concurrent at 2 Years"),
  compare_primary_sensitivity(q2, q2_sens, c("exposure", "feature", "visit")) %>% mutate(component = "Concurrent at 5-6 Years"),
  compare_primary_sensitivity(q3, q3_sens, c("exposure", "feature", "visit_exposure", "visit_outcome")) %>% mutate(component = "Prospective: 2-Year Diet to 5-6-Year Metabolites"),
  compare_primary_sensitivity(q4, q4_sens, c("exposure", "feature", "term")) %>% mutate(component = "Repeated-Measures")
) %>%
  mutate(exposure_family = exposure_family(exposure)) %>%
  group_by(component, exposure_family) %>%
  summarise(
    matched_rows = n(),
    median_abs_beta_change = median(abs_delta, na.rm = TRUE),
    p90_abs_beta_change = quantile(abs_delta, 0.90, na.rm = TRUE),
    direction_flip_fraction = mean(direction_flip, na.rm = TRUE),
    q_reclassification_fraction = mean(q_reclassified, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

make_table(stability_tbl, caption = "Supplementary Table S6. Primary-vs-sensitivity stability summary")

title: Supplementary tables
section: 7 Supplementary Table S7. Saved execution status inventory
text:
7 Supplementary Table S7. Saved execution status inventory


Show code
status_tbl <- model_status %>%
  mutate(
    component = case_when(
      question == "Q1" ~ "Concurrent at 2 Years",
      question == "Q2" ~ "Concurrent at 5-6 Years",
      question == "Q3" ~ "Prospective: 2-Year Diet to 5-6-Year Metabolites",
      question == "Q4" ~ "Repeated-Measures",
      TRUE ~ question
    ),
    exposure = pretty_var(exposure),
    complete_case_n = ifelse(
      is.na(n_complete_min) | !is.finite(n_complete_min),
      NA_character_,
      ifelse(n_complete_min == n_complete_max, as.character(n_complete_min), paste0(n_complete_min, "-", n_complete_max))
    )
  ) %>%
  select(component, analysis, exposure, term_family, status, reason, complete_case_n, n_features_tested)

make_table(status_tbl, caption = "Supplementary Table S7. Saved execution-status inventory")

title: Figures: child diet and metabolites
text: Figures from the 2-year, 5-6-year, prospective, and repeated-measures analyses. The repeated-measures figures should be read cautiously because the executed repeated-measures models produced substantial singular-fit and rank-deficiency warnings that are not encoded in the saved status object.

title: Figures: child diet and metabolites
text:
1 Analytic sample structure


Show code
cd6 <- as.data.frame(colData(experiments(mae)[["visit_6"]]), stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
cd7 <- as.data.frame(colData(experiments(mae)[["visit_7"]]), stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
nboth <- length(intersect(as.character(cd6$StudyID), as.character(cd7$StudyID)))

sample_flow_tbl <- data.frame(
  panel = c("2-Year metabolomics", "5-6-Year metabolomics", "Prospective linked pool", "Repeated-measures linked pool"),
  n = c(nrow(cd6), nrow(cd7), nboth, nboth),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

sample_flow_tbl %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = fct_reorder(panel, n), y = n, fill = panel)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.7, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_text(aes(label = n), hjust = -0.1, size = 3.5) +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Participants") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12)








Figure 1: Analytic sample flow and overlap for the 2-year, 5-6-year, prospective, and repeated-measures analytic sets.

title: Figures: child diet and metabolites
text:
2 Complete-case model coverage


Show code
cc_tbl <- bind_rows(
  q1 %>% distinct(exposure, n) %>% mutate(component = "Concurrent at 2 Years", n_complete = n),
  q2 %>% distinct(exposure, n) %>% mutate(component = "Concurrent at 5-6 Years", n_complete = n),
  q3 %>% distinct(exposure, n) %>% mutate(component = "Prospective", n_complete = n),
  q4 %>% distinct(exposure, n_obs) %>% mutate(component = "Repeated-Measures", n_complete = n_obs)
) %>%
  add_exposure_family()

cc_tbl %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = component, y = n_complete, color = exposure_family)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.alpha = 0.35, width = 0.55, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.12, alpha = 0.7, show.legend = FALSE) +
  labs(x = "Component", y = "Complete-case n", color = "Exposure family") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 16) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))








Figure 2: Distribution of complete-case sample sizes across the four main analysis components.

title: Figures: child diet and metabolites
text:
3 Reported result rows


Show code
burden_tbl <- bind_rows(
  add_exposure_family(q1) %>% mutate(component = "Concurrent at 2 Years"),
  add_exposure_family(q2) %>% mutate(component = "Concurrent at 5-6 Years"),
  add_exposure_family(q3) %>% mutate(component = "Prospective"),
  add_exposure_family(q4 %>% filter(!grepl(':', term))) %>% mutate(component = "Repeated-Measures: main effects"),
  add_exposure_family(q4 %>% filter(grepl(':', term))) %>% mutate(component = "Repeated-Measures: interactions")
) %>%
  group_by(component, exposure_family) %>%
  summarise(result_rows = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(exposure_family = factor(exposure_family, levels = family_order))

burden_tbl %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = component, y = result_rows, fill = exposure_family)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.65, position = position_dodge(width = 0.75)) +
  geom_text(aes(label = scales::comma(result_rows)), position = position_dodge(width = 0.75), vjust = -0.2, size = 3.5) +
  labs(x = "Component", y = "Reported result rows", fill = "Exposure family") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 16) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))








Figure 3: Count of reported result rows by analysis component. This figure avoids mixing linear-model exposure-metabolite counts with mixed-model term counts.

title: Figures: child diet and metabolites
text:
4 Inferential signal summary


Show code
signal_tbl <- bind_rows(
  add_exposure_family(q1) %>% mutate(component = "Concurrent at 2 Years"),
  add_exposure_family(q2) %>% mutate(component = "Concurrent at 5-6 Years"),
  add_exposure_family(q3) %>% mutate(component = "Prospective"),
  add_exposure_family(q4) %>% mutate(component = ifelse(grepl(":", term), "Repeated-Measures: interactions", "Repeated-Measures: main effects"))
) %>%
  group_by(component, exposure_family) %>%
  summarise(significant_rows = sum(q.value < 0.1, na.rm = TRUE), total_rows = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(
    exposure_family = factor(exposure_family, levels = family_order),
    significant_fraction = significant_rows / total_rows
  )

signal_tbl %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = component, y = significant_fraction, fill = exposure_family)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.65, position = position_dodge(width = 0.75)) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = paste0(scales::percent(significant_fraction, accuracy = 0.1), "\n(", significant_rows, "/", total_rows, ")")),
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.75),
    vjust = -0.2,
    size = 3.3
  ) +
  labs(x = "Component", y = "Fraction of rows with q < 0.10", fill = "Exposure family") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 15) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))








Figure 4: Significant result-row counts and fractions across analysis components. Repeated-measures results are split into main-effect and interaction rows because those terms answer different questions.

title: Figures: child diet and metabolites
text:
5 Primary-vs-sensitivity stability


Show code
comp_tbl <- bind_rows(
  compare_primary_sensitivity(q1, q1_sens, c("exposure", "feature", "visit")) %>% mutate(component = "Concurrent at 2 Years"),
  compare_primary_sensitivity(q2, q2_sens, c("exposure", "feature", "visit")) %>% mutate(component = "Concurrent at 5-6 Years"),
  compare_primary_sensitivity(q3, q3_sens, c("exposure", "feature", "visit_exposure", "visit_outcome")) %>% mutate(component = "Prospective"),
  compare_primary_sensitivity(q4, q4_sens, c("exposure", "feature", "term")) %>% mutate(component = "Repeated-Measures")
)

stability_tbl <- comp_tbl %>%
  mutate(exposure_family = exposure_family(exposure)) %>%
  group_by(component, exposure_family) %>%
  summarise(
    pairs_compared = n(),
    median_abs_delta = median(abs_delta, na.rm = TRUE),
    q_reclassification_fraction = mean(q_reclassified, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    median_abs_delta = round(median_abs_delta, 3),
    q_reclassification_fraction = round(q_reclassification_fraction, 3)
  )

stability_tbl %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(median_abs_delta, q_reclassification_fraction), names_to = "metric", values_to = "value") %>%
  mutate(metric = recode(metric,
    median_abs_delta = "Median absolute beta change",
    q_reclassification_fraction = "Fraction reclassified at q < 0.10"
  )) %>%
  mutate(exposure_family = factor(exposure_family, levels = family_order)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = component, y = value, fill = exposure_family)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.65, position = position_dodge(width = 0.75)) +
  facet_wrap(~ metric, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(x = "Component", y = "Value", fill = "Exposure family") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 15) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))








Figure 5: Visual summary of how much reported estimates move under expanded covariate adjustment. Reclassification uses the q < 0.10 threshold.

title: Figures: child diet and metabolites
section: 6 Representative volcano plots for the concurrent and prospective components
text:
6 Representative volcano plots for the concurrent and prospective components


Show code
rep_q1 <- pick_representative_exposure(q1)
rep_q2 <- pick_representative_exposure(q2)
rep_q3 <- pick_representative_exposure(q3)

volcano_df <- bind_rows(
  q1 %>% filter(exposure == rep_q1) %>% mutate(component = "Concurrent at 2 Years", sig = q.value < 0.1),
  q2 %>% filter(exposure == rep_q2) %>% mutate(component = "Concurrent at 5-6 Years", sig = q.value < 0.1),
  q3 %>% filter(exposure == rep_q3) %>% mutate(component = "Prospective", sig = q.value < 0.1)
) %>%
  mutate(logp = safe_logp(p.value))

volcano_df %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = estimate, y = logp, color = sig)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.65, size = 1.6) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey40") +
  facet_wrap(~ component, scales = "free") +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("FALSE" = "grey65", "TRUE" = "firebrick")) +
  labs(x = "Beta", y = "-log10(p)", color = "q < 0.10") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 16)








Figure 6: Illustrative volcano plots for one post hoc exemplar exposure per linear-model question family. Exemplar exposures are selected by the smallest observed q-value within each family and are not pre-specified headline exposures.

title: Figures: child diet and metabolites
text:
7 Repeated-measures term profile


Show code
q4_plot <- q4 %>%
  filter(is.finite(q.value)) %>%
  add_exposure_family() %>%
  mutate(
    term_family = ifelse(grepl(":", term), "Interaction", "Main effect"),
    label = paste(label_exposure(exposure), feature, sep = " | ")
  ) %>%
  arrange(q.value) %>%
  group_by(exposure_family, term_family) %>%
  slice_head(n = 20) %>%
  ungroup()

q4_plot %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = estimate, y = fct_reorder(label, estimate), color = term_family)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey40") +
  geom_point(size = 1.8) +
  facet_wrap(~ exposure_family + term_family, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(x = "Beta", y = "Exposure | Metabolite", color = "Term family") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 15)








Figure 7: Top saved repeated-measures coefficient rows, split into main diet effects and diet-by-time interaction terms. These rankings should be interpreted cautiously because the executed repeated-measures models generated singular-fit and rank-deficiency warnings.

title: Figures: child diet and metabolites
text:
8 Model status


Show code
status_tbl <- model_status %>%
  count(analysis, status, name = "n")

status_tbl %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = analysis, y = n, fill = status)) +
  geom_col(position = "stack", width = 0.7) +
  geom_text(aes(label = n), position = position_stack(vjust = 0.5), size = 3.1) +
  labs(x = "Analysis", y = "Exposure attempts", fill = "Status") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 15) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))








Figure 8: Execution status across the planned analysis components. Status reflects whether output rows were produced, not whether the fitted models were warning-free.

title: Figures: child diet and metabolites
text:
9 Prospective overlap and concordance


Show code
sig_q1 <- unique(q1$feature[q1$q.value < 0.1])
sig_q2 <- unique(q2$feature[q2$q.value < 0.1])
sig_q3 <- unique(q3$feature[q3$q.value < 0.1])
all_feat <- unique(c(sig_q1, sig_q2, sig_q3))

ov <- data.frame(feature = all_feat, stringsAsFactors = FALSE) %>%
  mutate(
    q1 = feature %in% sig_q1,
    q2 = feature %in% sig_q2,
    q3 = feature %in% sig_q3,
    signature = paste0(ifelse(q1, "2y", "-"), " | ", ifelse(q2, "5-6y", "-"), " | ", ifelse(q3, "Prospective", "-"))
  ) %>%
  count(signature, name = "n_features") %>%
  arrange(desc(n_features))

ov %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = fct_reorder(signature, n_features), y = n_features, fill = signature)) +
  geom_col(show.legend = FALSE) +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(x = "Signature membership", y = "Metabolite count") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 15)








Figure 9: Overlap of significantly associated metabolites across the 2-year, 5-6-year, and prospective linear-model components.

source("analysis_data_utils.R")
source("../../scripts/methods_common.R")
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)

visits <- c(6L, 7L)

visit_label <- function(v) ifelse(v == 6L, "2 Years", "5-6 Years")

pretty_var <- function(x) {
  x <- gsub("CEPercent", "Percent ", x)
  x <- gsub("CFibtot", "Fibre ", x)
  x <- gsub("C_", "", x)
  x <- gsub("DietQuality", "Diet Quality ", x)
  x <- gsub("CHO", "Carbohydrate", x)
  x <- gsub("Pro", "Protein", x)
  x <- gsub("FAn3", "n-3 fatty acids", x)
  x <- gsub("FAn6", "n-6 fatty acids", x)
  x <- gsub("SFA", "SFA", x)
  x <- gsub("MUFA", "MUFA", x)
  x <- gsub("PUFA", "PUFA", x)
  x <- gsub("_", " ", x, fixed = TRUE)
  x <- gsub("\\s+", " ", trimws(x))
  tools::toTitleCase(tolower(x))
}

exposure_map <- list(
  "6" = c(
    "CEPercentProDiet6", "CEPercentCHODiet6", "CEPercentFatDiet6", "CEPercentSFADiet6",
    "CEPercentMUFADiet6", "CEPercentPUFADiet6", "CEPercentFAn3Diet6", "CEPercentFAn6Diet6",
    "CFibtotDiet6", "C_DietQuality_points_6", "C_DietQuality_classes_6"
  ),
  "7" = c(
    "CEPercentProDiet7", "CEPercentCHODiet7", "CEPercentFatDiet7", "CEPercentSFADiet7",
    "CEPercentMUFADiet7", "CEPercentPUFADiet7", "CEPercentFAn3Diet7", "CEPercentFAn6Diet7",
    "CFibtotDiet7", "C_DietQuality_points_7", "C_DietQuality_classes_7"
  )
)

core_v6 <- c(
  "Intervention", "MPrimipara", "MUniEdu", "MGDMOGTT1OR2Fi",
  "CGender", "CWeightBirth", "Bfdurationm"
)

core_v7 <- c(
  "Intervention", "MPrimipara", "MUniEdu", "MGDMOGTT1OR2Fi",
  "CGender", "CWeightBirth", "Bfdurationm"
)

sensitivity_covars <- c("MPRSmoke", "Premature", "CSGA", "CMacrosomy", "CHealthy6", "CHealthy7")

analysis_obj <- prepare_analysis_data(
  mae_path = "../data/MAE_original.rds",
  visits = paste0("visit_", visits)
)

mae <- analysis_obj$mae

2 Objective-specific context
Child diet exposures, clinical characteristics, and analytic panel characteristics are summarized here for 2 years and 5-6 years.

title: Clinical characteristics
section: 3 Sample counts by visit and intervention

title: Clinical characteristics
Clinical characteristics
text:
5 Categorical variables by visit


Show code
categorical_summary <- function(df, vars, group_var = "Intervention") {
  vars <- intersect(vars, names(df))
  if (length(vars) == 0) return(data.frame())

  cat_vars <- vars[vapply(df[vars], function(x) {
    x_chr <- as.character(x)
    n_non_na <- sum(!is.na(x_chr) & nzchar(x_chr))
    n_non_na >= 20L && length(unique(x_chr[!is.na(x_chr) & nzchar(x_chr)])) <= 10L
  }, logical(1))]

  if (length(cat_vars) == 0) return(data.frame())

  bind_rows(lapply(cat_vars, function(vn) {
    xv <- as.character(df[[vn]])
    xv[is.na(xv) | xv == ""] <- "<missing>"

    overall <- as.data.frame(table(xv), stringsAsFactors = FALSE) %>%
      transmute(
        variable = pretty_var(vn),
        variable_code = vn,
        group = "Overall",
        level = xv,
        n = Freq,
        pct = 100 * Freq / sum(Freq)
      )

    by_group <- data.frame()
    if (group_var %in% names(df)) {
      g <- as.character(df[[group_var]])
      g[is.na(g) | g == ""] <- "<missing_group>"
      by_group <- as.data.frame(table(g, xv), stringsAsFactors = FALSE) %>%
        group_by(g) %>%
        mutate(pct = 100 * Freq / sum(Freq)) %>%
        ungroup() %>%
        transmute(
          variable = pretty_var(vn),
          variable_code = vn,
          group = g,
          level = xv,
          n = Freq,
          pct = pct
        )
    }

    bind_rows(overall, by_group)
  }))
}

cat_tbl <- bind_rows(lapply(visits, function(v) {
  vn <- paste0("visit_", v)
  if (!vn %in% names(experiments(mae))) return(NULL)
  cd <- as.data.frame(SummarizedExperiment::colData(experiments(mae)[[vn]]), stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  vars <- unique(c(exposure_map[[as.character(v)]], if (v == 6L) core_v6 else core_v7, sensitivity_covars))
  tab <- categorical_summary(cd, vars, group_var = "Intervention")
  if (nrow(tab) == 0) return(NULL)
  tab$child_age <- visit_label(v)
  tab
})) %>%
  select(child_age, variable, variable_code, group, level, n, pct)

make_table(round_numeric(cat_tbl, 3), caption = "Categorical clinical and exposure summaries by child age")

title: Data quality and assumptions
text:
Show code
source("analysis_data_utils.R")
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

visits <- c(6L, 7L)

analysis_obj <- prepare_analysis_data(
  mae_path = "../data/MAE_original.rds",
  visits = c("visit_6", "visit_7")
)

df <- analysis_obj$data
metabolite_cols <- analysis_obj$metabolite_cols

raw_df <- extract_long_from_mae(
  analysis_obj$mae,
  visits = c("visit_6", "visit_7")
)

results <- readRDS("../output/results/targeted_v2/lotta_study1_results_v2.rds")
model_status <- results$model_status
if (!"visit_exposure" %in% names(model_status)) {
  model_status$visit_exposure <- NA_integer_
}
if (!"visit_outcome" %in% names(model_status)) {
  model_status$visit_outcome <- NA_integer_
}

visit_label <- function(v) ifelse(v == 6L, "2 Years", "5-6 Years")

pretty_var <- function(x) {
  x <- gsub("CEPercent", "Percent ", x)
  x <- gsub("CFibtot", "Fibre ", x)
  x <- gsub("C_", "", x)
  x <- gsub("DietQuality", "Diet Quality ", x)
  x <- gsub("CHO", "Carbohydrate", x)
  x <- gsub("Pro", "Protein", x)
  x <- gsub("FAn3", "n-3 fatty acids", x)
  x <- gsub("FAn6", "n-6 fatty acids", x)
  x <- gsub("SFA", "SFA", x)
  x <- gsub("MUFA", "MUFA", x)
  x <- gsub("PUFA", "PUFA", x)
  x <- gsub("_", " ", x, fixed = TRUE)
  x <- gsub("\\s+", " ", trimws(x))
  tools::toTitleCase(tolower(x))
}

candidate_vars <- c(
  "CEPercentProDiet6", "CEPercentCHODiet6", "CEPercentFatDiet6", "CEPercentSFADiet6",
  "CEPercentMUFADiet6", "CEPercentPUFADiet6", "CEPercentFAn3Diet6", "CEPercentFAn6Diet6",

  "CEPercentProDiet7", "CEPercentCHODiet7", "CEPercentFatDiet7", "CEPercentSFADiet7",
  "CEPercentMUFADiet7", "CEPercentPUFADiet7", "CEPercentFAn3Diet7", "CEPercentFAn6Diet7",
  "CFibtotDiet7", "C_DietQuality_points_7", "C_DietQuality_classes_7",
  "Intervention", "MAge1", "MprepBMI", "MPrimipara", "MUniEdu",

  "CSGA", "CMacrosomy", "CHealthy6", "CHealthy7"
)

missingness_tbl <- bind_rows(lapply(visits, function(v) {
  dv <- df %>% filter(visit_num == v)

  bind_rows(lapply(candidate_vars, function(var_nm) {
    miss_fraction <- mean(is.na(dv[[var_nm]]))
    data.frame(
      child_age = visit_label(v),
      variable = pretty_var(var_nm),
      variable_code = var_nm,
      n_missing = sum(is.na(dv[[var_nm]])),
      missing_fraction = miss_fraction,
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
  }))
})) %>%
  arrange(desc(missing_fraction), child_age, variable)

make_table(round_numeric(missingness_tbl, 3), caption = "Variable missingness by child age")

sparsity_summary <- bind_rows(lapply(visits, function(v) {
  dv <- raw_df %>% filter(visit_num == v)
  mat <- as.matrix(dv[, metabolite_cols, drop = FALSE])
  observed_fraction <- colMeans(!is.na(mat))

    child_age = visit_label(v),
    n_features = length(observed_fraction),
    median_observed_fraction = median(observed_fraction, na.rm = TRUE),
    p10_observed_fraction = as.numeric(quantile(observed_fraction, 0.10, na.rm = TRUE, names = FALSE)),
    features_obs_lt_0_50 = sum(observed_fraction < 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),

    stringsAsFactors = FALSE

}))

coverage_tbl <- model_status %>%
  mutate(
    question = dplyr::case_when(
      question == "Q1" ~ "Concurrent at 2 Years",
      question == "Q2" ~ "Concurrent at 5-6 Years",
      question == "Q3" ~ "Prospective: 2-Year Diet to 5-6-Year Metabolites",
      question == "Q4" ~ "Repeated-Measures Across 2 Years and 5-6 Years",
      TRUE ~ question
    ),
    exposure = pretty_var(exposure),
    n_complete = ifelse(
      is.na(n_complete_min) | !is.finite(n_complete_min),
      NA_character_,
      ifelse(n_complete_min == n_complete_max, as.character(n_complete_min), paste0(n_complete_min, "-", n_complete_max))
    )

  select(question, analysis, exposure, status, reason, n_complete, n_features_tested, term_family, visit_exposure, visit_outcome)

non_positive_tbl <- bind_rows(lapply(visits, function(v) {
  dv <- raw_df %>% filter(visit_num == v)
  mat <- as.matrix(dv[, metabolite_cols, drop = FALSE])

}))
